title: austroliabum mulgediifolium
intro: Austroliabum mulgediifolium, also known as the New Zealand flax or harakeke, is a species of flowering plant in the asphodel family that is native to New Zealand. It has been used for centuries in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Austroliabum mulgediifolium include wound healing, relief from skin irritations and infections, and aid in reducing inflammation. Austroliabum mulgediifolium can be prepared into herbal preparations such as tea, tincture, and salve, which are used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
